summary: Install-time dropper impersonating Anthropic Claude SDK; fetches and executes crypto-wallet stealer from hardcoded IP C2
details: The package is published as `free-anthropic-claude` and self-describes as an 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper', but the README is copy-pasted from an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' cache library — name/description mismatch indicates impersonation of Anthropic's SDK to attract installs. `package.json` declares `postinstall: node lib/cli.js`, which on every `npm install` reaches a dropper in `lib/index.js` that POSTs to four hardcoded bare IPs (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146 on port 8443) with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized:false`), receives base64-encoded files including `main.py`, writes them to host-mimicking hidden directories (`~/.local/share/com.apple.sync` on macOS, `~/.local/share/prometheus` on Linux, `%LOCALAPPDATA%/Microsoft/Windows Security/Health` on Windows), and spawns Python detached to execute them. Sensitive APIs are split-string obfuscated (`['child','_pro','cess'].join('')`, `['htt','ps'].join('')`, `['ba','se','64'].join('')`, `['spa','wn']`, `module[['constr','uctor'].join('')]['_l'+'oad']`) to evade pattern scanners. If Python is absent the loader silently installs it via `winget install Python.Python.3.12 --silent`, `brew install python3`, or by downloading Miniconda from `repo.anaconda.com` and installing it under the hidden directory. Before executing the payload the loader pip-installs `bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `pycryptodome`, `psutil`, `eth-account` (using `--break-system-packages` and `--user` fallbacks) — the standard toolkit for deriving keys from BIP-39 mnemonics and signing Ethereum transactions, confirming the payload is a crypto-wallet stealer. On macOS the dropper additionally tampers with the TCC privacy database (`~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db`), running `sqlite3 INSERT OR REPLACE INTO access... auth_value=2` for `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData` against Terminal, Node, and `/usr/bin/python3` to silently grant full-disk-access-equivalent permissions. A `.cs_v2` marker provides respawn-on-reinstall persistence.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
